[pattern] [warning] Generated artifact HENCH_CALLGRAPH_FINDINGS.md is committed but has no CI regeneration-and-diff guard; it can silently go stale after hench source changes
[anti-pattern] [warning] hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs produces HENCH_CALLGRAPH_FINDINGS.md but has no fail-fast guard that detects missing or stale input artifacts; silent success on a cold checkout produces a misleading (empty or stale) report with no error signal.

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs generates the HENCH_CALLGRAPH_FINDINGS.md report, forming a producer/consumer pair that documents architectural analysis findings
- test-zone-consistency.mjs validates that zone IDs in sourcevision output are stable across runs, acting as a CI guard for zone naming regressions
- These scripts are outside any package and have no coupling to production code, which is correct — they're diagnostic tools that read output artifacts rather than participating in the build
- Scripts have perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling, indicating they are self-contained analysis tools that correctly avoid importing production modules.
- HENCH_CALLGRAPH_FINDINGS.md is a generated artifact committed alongside the script that produced it; a CI step could regenerate and diff it to catch stale findings.
- test-zone-consistency.mjs duplicates some concern with the e2e zone's architecture-policy tests; clarifying which file owns the canonical zone-consistency assertion would reduce overlap.
- dev-analysis-scripts is the only non-test zone in this batch with coupling=0, forming a distinct 'diagnostic tier' that reads build artifacts without participating in any build graph — this is a valid and intentional pattern but undocumented
- Committing generated markdown (HENCH_CALLGRAPH_FINDINGS.md) alongside the script that produces it creates a latent staleness risk; no CI step currently regenerates and diffs the artifact to catch it going out of date
- Generated artifact HENCH_CALLGRAPH_FINDINGS.md is committed but has no CI regeneration-and-diff guard; it can silently go stale after hench source changes
- hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs reads hench source or output artifacts at runtime but has no declared dependency on a build step — running it on a cold checkout or after hench source changes without rebuilding produces silently stale output rather than a clear failure
- The three scripts in this zone have no shared entry point or runner; there is no documented command that executes all three in sequence, making it easy to forget to run one after a change that invalidates its output
- hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs produces HENCH_CALLGRAPH_FINDINGS.md but has no fail-fast guard that detects missing or stale input artifacts; silent success on a cold checkout produces a misleading (empty or stale) report with no error signal.
- [call graph] 6 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
